rows.text: moonlight spills across the empty station / one suitcase waits for dawn
rows.explanation: Deliberate line breaks, vivid imagery (moonlight spilling, empty station), and poetic devices (personification with waiting suitcase) indicate poetry.

rows.text: three sparrows argue / on the wire above rainwater / city morning hymn
rows.explanation: Clear poetic structure with three lines, intentional line breaks, vivid imagery (sparrows, wire, rainwater), and metaphorical language (morning hymn). Follows haiku-adjacent form with sensory details and contemplative tone.

rows.text: I folded winter into a paper crane and let it go
Tonight the ocean writes cursive on the sand
between two heartbeats / the kettle begins singing / and the room softens
rows.explanation: Clear poetic structure with three lines and intentional line breaks. Uses vivid imagery (heartbeats, singing kettle, softening room) and employs metaphorical language typical of haiku or free verse poetry.

rows.text: Your name drifts like pollen through my open window
A candle keeps the dark from feeling lonely
The bridge hums gently under tired commuter feet
I planted silence and harvested a field of stars
Old books breathe dust and thunder when they open
rain stitches silver threads through the pine trees
Midnight leans against the balcony and listens
I keep a lighthouse in my chest for lost days
dandelion clocks / count down to summer again / children chase the wind
rows.explanation: Clear haiku structure with three lines, seasonal imagery, line breaks, and poetic language depicting a scene with metaphorical elements.

rows.text: My shadow learns your language at sunset
A cracked mug still holds enough warmth for hope
snow on the mailbox / each letter unread but glowing / like tiny moons
rows.explanation: Clear poetic structure with intentional line breaks, vivid imagery, and a metaphorical comparison (letters glowing like moons). The compressed, imagistic language and sensory details are characteristic of poetry.

rows.text: I borrowed thunder to say what I could not
The alley cat wears dawn like a velvet coat
Every goodbye leaves a feather on the stairs
New internship posted for data science students at Northstar Robotics. Apply by Friday.
Breaking: City council meeting starts at 6pm in the downtown civic center.
Looking for 2 roommates near campus. DM for details.
Weekly grocery list: milk, rice, eggs, onions, olive oil, detergent.
GPU cluster maintenance tonight from 11pm to 1am UTC.
Hacker News thread: What are your favorite terminal tools in 2026?
Just published our paper on retrieval-augmented generation. Preprint link in bio.
Traffic advisory: northbound lanes closed due to construction until Monday.
Reminder to submit your assignment before 11:59pm.
Discount code SPRING26 now live for all annual plans.
rows.explanation: Promotional announcement with discount code and product information, no poetic devices or line breaks.

rows.text: Join our meetup this Thursday for startup founders and engineers.
System status: all services operational.
Can anyone recommend a good dentist near La Jolla?
I am selling my bike. Recently tuned. Pickup only.
Lecture moved to room 210 due to projector issues.
Please complete the onboarding form and upload ID documents.
What laptop should I buy for machine learning class?
Our API now supports streaming responses in beta.
Event announcement: beach cleanup this Saturday at 9am.
The package arrived and I signed for it at noon.
haiku in my pocket / coffee cold beside the code / sunrise on my screen
rows.explanation: Clear haiku structure with three lines, intentional line breaks, poetic imagery (haiku, coffee, sunrise), and a contemplative tone typical of poetry.

rows.text: sonnet of sidewalks / evening traffic in couplets / headlights rhyme with rain
rows.explanation: Intentional line breaks, poetic form references (sonnet, couplets), and metaphorical imagery (headlights rhyming with rain) clearly indicate poetic writing.

rows.text: a verse for tomorrow: be kind when the world is loud
rows.explanation: The phrase 'a verse for tomorrow' explicitly signals poetic intent. The content uses figurative language ('when the world is loud') and conveys emotional/philosophical meaning typical of poetry, despite lacking traditional line breaks.

rows.text: my stanza is short / but it carries all my grief / into morning light
rows.explanation: Clear line breaks, poetic structure with three lines, metaphorical language ('carries all my grief'), and imagery ('morning light') are characteristic of poetry.

rows.text: She wrote a small ode to tomatoes and summer soup
rows.explanation: This is a prose statement describing someone writing a poem, rather than being a poem itself. It lacks line breaks, poetic structure, and direct poetic language.

rows.text: This post is definitely not a poem, just saying hi to everyone.
rows.explanation: Casual greeting with explicit statement that it is not a poem. No poetic devices, line breaks, or imagery present.

rows.text: Poetry club meets at 5pm in room B12. New members welcome.
rows.explanation: Announcement of a club meeting with practical details (time, location, membership info). No poetic devices, line breaks, or imagery present.

rows.text: Haiku challenge deadline extended to next week.
rows.explanation: Administrative announcement about a deadline extension. While it mentions haiku, it is not itself a poem—it lacks poetic structure, imagery, or line breaks characteristic of poetry.

rows.text: Rhyme zone website is down right now for maintenance.
rows.explanation: Straightforward informational statement about website maintenance. No poetic devices, line breaks, imagery, or figurative language present.

rows.text: I bought a book called Modern Poetry but have not started reading it.
rows.explanation: This is a straightforward statement about purchasing a book. It lacks poetic devices such as line breaks, imagery, metaphor, or rhythmic structure. The content is conversational and literal rather than artistic.
